title: React Native Engineer
description:
**Note:** 
 During July most of us is away, so expect longer response times. Most realistically by mid August we will start responding. But feel free to apply now.
   

  

**Gimi**
 We're a small team building the financial infrastructure banks use to teach kids and teens how to handle money — real accounts, real payments, real habits. As a React Native Engineer you'd be joining our mobile team at a point where the work is concrete and shipping fast: right now that means our end-to-end allowance system — the weekly pocket money flow that runs for thousands of families, from parent setup to the ledger that tracks every transfer.
   

  

****What you'd actually be doing****
* Building and shipping features in our React Native app — not just maintaining, actual new product surface
* Working closely with the person next to you (product, design, or another engineer) rather than waiting for a spec to land
* Making real calls on tradeoffs — e.g. when an edge case in a recurring payment flow breaks, deciding how much complexity to absorb quietly vs. surface to the user
* Using AI tools as a core part of how you build — from scoping to shipping — not as an occasional shortcut
* Writing code your teammates can pick up without you needing to explain it in Slack afterward


**What We're Looking For**
* You've shipped real React Native apps to production — show us, don't just tell us (a GitHub link, an app you've worked on, whatever proves it)
* You're comfortable with JS/TypeScript and can hold your own around Node
* You already build with AI tools day to day and have a real opinion on where they help and where they don't — this matters more to us than most other lines on this list
* You can explain a technical decision you made and why, including one you'd make differently now


****Nice to have****
* Native iOS or Android experience
* CI/CD experience
* Firebase
* Fintech, or any regulated/compliance-heavy industry


****To apply****
 Skip the generic cover letter — instead, tell us about a bug or technical problem you found genuinely satisfying to solve, and what made it hard.
   

  

**Note:** 
 During July most of us is away, so expect longer response times. Most realistically by mid August we will start responding. But feel free to apply now.

title: Fullstack Developer (TypeScript)
description:
At Qred, we’re building the world's best bank for small businesses. Since launching 11 years ago, we’ve grown from startup to profitable fintech scale-up, now generating over 1 billion SEK in annual revenue and supporting 50,000+ entrepreneurs across Northern Europe. We combine smart technology, real data, and human judgment to make financing simple, fast, and fair. With bold growth plans and strong momentum across multiple markets, we’re now looking for Fullstack Developers for several teams for our next phase of growth.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Fullstack Developer at Qred, you will take end-to-end ownership of features that directly impact our users, fluidly transitioning between frontend and backend systems based on team priorities. You will bridge the gap between technical execution and business impact by building scalable financial systems while ensuring a seamless, high-performance user experience. This mission-driven role offers the autonomy to solve complex engineering problems and deliver immediate value to our product ecosystem.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Build secure, scalable backend services, web/mobile user interfaces, and third-party integrations.
* Collaborate with designers and peer engineers to plan efficient data flows, endpoints, and optimal client experiences.
* Deploy, test, and monitor production code while maintaining proper logging, tracing, and system health dashboards.
* Drive technical initiatives within our client platforms, ensuring shared frontend practices remain scalable and unified.
* Mitigate technical risk by adhering to compliant engineering practices and managing a disciplined workflow to maintain delivery momentum.


 Our stack: AWS Serverless, Typescript, Node.js, React, React Native, Claude Code, Grafana, Github Actions.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
 We are seeking structured professionals who thrives on architectural variety and can drive alignment across both frontend and backend domains. You possess strong self-leadership, manage your workflow by focusing on closing active tasks, and avoid the efficiency traps of multi-tasking. You are motivated by building scalable frameworks and establishing high engineering maturity within a fast-paced fintech ecosystem.
   

  

**Qualifications**
* Several years of professional experience in software engineering using TypeScript.
* Strong knowledge of modern cloud environments, secure data transferring, and building scalable web or mobile client platforms.
* Practical exposure to using AI models, LLMs, or building automated agentic workflows.


**Why Qred?**
 This is the place to be if you’re looking for a place to grow. Qred is growing fast, and our Qredsters along with it. With a non-bureaucratic organization and delegated responsibilities, we make sure there’s a short path from idea to action. In addition to our great culture, you get to work with the latest cutting-edge techniques, full ownership, and last but not least a bunch of great competent colleagues to learn from!
   

  

**One Last Thing**
 This is a full-time, permanent position based in our headquarters in Stockholm. We believe our culture thrives when we work together, which is why we have an office-first approach. To balance this with some flexibility, we have the option of working remotely one day per week. We review applications on a rolling basis and while the start date is flexible, the right candidate can join us immediately.
   

  

*Qred celebrates diversity and does not discriminate based on ethnicity, religion, national origin, gender, sexua* 
*l orientation, age, disability status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law.*
 Qred is an international FinTech scale-up that specializes in digital financing solutions for small businesses, with operations in the Nordics, Benelux, Germany and Brazil. We are passionate about helping small businesses thrive and grow, by providing them with business loans and credit cards. Right now we’re in an exciting phase of our journey that focuses on growth through smart product development and technology fueled by international expansion.

title: Backend Software Engineer
description:
**About us** 




 Ingrid was born out of a simple belief: the energy transition must move faster and work smarter. We're pioneers at the forefront of the global energy transition — pushing boundaries, turning bold ideas into reality, and solving the problems that matter most.
 



  





 The INtelligent GRID of tomorrow needs to absorb and release more power. We develop, build and operate high-performance batteries — the lungs of modern energy infrastructure — enabling a more flexible and resilient energy supply across Europe. With 500 MW already deployed across the Nordics and rapid expansion into continental Europe, we manage, optimise and trade both our own assets and those of third parties through our sophisticated tech platform, making every battery — and the entire grid — stronger.
 



  





**Watch our film** 




  





**About the role** 




 Software and smart solutions are at the core of how we tackle the toughest bottlenecks in the power system. The platform our Ingrid Technology team builds makes real-time trading decisions, balances the grid and optimises assets – running at scale, around the clock, against live energy markets where timing and reliability decide the outcome.
 



  





 We're looking for a Backend Engineer to join the Platforms team - a group that owns the Cloud, Data, and Applications platforms. You'll go deep in the backend – real-time systems, event-driven services and the infrastructure they run on – with the only limit being your own curiosity and ambition.
 



  





 This is a hands-on role with real ownership: what you design, you build, ship and run. You'll work close to trading, market intelligence and asset operations. You’ll deploy often and see your impact quickly.
 



  





**What you'll do** 



* Go deep in one of our key domains. Optimisation & Trade, Delivery & Steering, or the Partner Experience Platform — and grow with it as we scale.
* Design, build and maintain production-grade backend services. In Python (FastAPI, Pydantic, SQLModel/SQLAlchemy, Alembic migrations), with RESTful and event-driven APIs, deployed as containerised micro services in the cloud.
* Build real-time, event-driven systems. Our services react in real time to continuously trading markets, a constant flow of surrounding data, and the physical steering of our assets — you'll design Pub/Sub event flows that stay predictable when messages arrive late, twice or not at all.
* Design and evolve application databases. PostgreSQL, Redis, BigQuery and S3 — schema design, migrations and query optimisation.
* Own the path from commit to production. CI/CD, containerisation, monitoring and observability — with security and auth built in from the start, not as an afterthought.



  





**Your background** 



* Deep backend experience in Python. Production services with modern tooling – FastAPI or equivalent, type-safe patterns – and you know your way around relational databases.
* Experience with event-driven or distributed systems. You've worked with message queues or event streams – Pub/Sub, Kafka or similar – and know the trade-offs they bring.
* At home in the cloud, ideally GCP. Or another major provider with a curiosity to learn – you don't just deploy services, you keep them monitored, observable and healthy in production.
* Experience with the energy sector is meritable, but not required. We'll get you up to speed on the domain.
* AI-native and genuinely keen on modern AI tooling, using it to sharpen your own workflow and helping colleagues get the most out of theirs.



  





**Your personal skills** 




 In addition to your qualifications we believe that below skills will make you a star in the Ingrid Tech team:
 


* Solution-oriented with ability to focus on finding practical solutions balancing speed and technical debt effectively.
* Curious and a natural inclination to explore new technologies, methodologies, and approaches, driving continuous improvement; actively seizing opportunities and taking initiative.
* Flexible in fast-paced start-up environment and willingness to test out ideas, fail fast and take on new tasks when needed.



  





**Why join us?** 




 At Ingrid, we're pioneers at the forefront of the global energy transition — pushing boundaries, turning bold ideas into reality, and solving what matters most. Our culture is built on Bold Impact · Curious Grit · Inspiring Collaboration. We believe a thriving organisation runs on trust, growth, and learning from mistakes — and that wellbeing matters as much as work. From lunch & learns to social activities, we make space to connect, recharge, and thrive together.
 



  





**Next step** 




 We’ll make selections on an ongoing basis and finalise the recruitment as soon as we’ve found the perfect match. This process will run over summer vacations and we will keep everyone involved updated on our expected timeline.
 



  





 We look forward to hearing from you!
 



  





 /Team Ingrid

title: Software Engineer
description:
We’re hiring a Software Engineer at PassportReader.
 



  





 PassportReader builds infrastructure for high-assurance identity verification using government-signed data from passports, ID cards, and digital wallets.
 



  





 Our product combines NFC chip reading, cryptographic document verification, mobile SDKs, APIs, face matching, liveness, and emerging EUDI wallet flows into one developer-friendly platform.
 



  





 We’re looking for a cracked engineer who wants to work close to the metal and close to the product.
 



  





 You’ll work on things like:
 



 • Native iOS and Android SDKs
 



 • NFC communication with passports and national ID cards
 



 • ICAO 9303 / eMRTD chip reading
 



 • Cryptographic verification of government-issued credentials
 



 • EUDI wallet and digital credential integrations
 



 • Backend APIs and developer tooling
 



 • Reliability, performance, and security across the full verification flow
 



  





 This role is a good fit if you enjoy building systems where correctness matters. Identity verification is not CRUD. The hard parts are protocol details, mobile edge cases, cryptography, document formats, hardware behavior, and making all of it feel simple for developers and end users.
 



  





 You might be a fit if you have strong experience with some of:
 



 • C++
 



 • Swift / iOS
 



 • Kotlin / Android
 



 • TypeScript / backend APIs
 



 • Cryptography, PKI, certificates, or digital signatures
 



 • NFC, smart cards, passports, eID cards, or mobile wallets
 



 • Security-sensitive infrastructure
 



 • Building SDKs or developer-facing products
 



  





 You do not need to know everything already. But you should be comfortable going deep, reading specifications, debugging strange edge cases, and owning important parts of the system end to end.
 



  





 We’re a small team, so this is a high-impact role. You’ll help shape the core product, architecture, engineering culture, and how high-assurance identity verification works across passports, ID cards, and digital wallets.
 



  





 Location: Stockholm / remote-friendly within Europe.

 Ingrid was born out of a simple belief: the energy transition must move faster and work smarter. We're pioneers at the forefront of the global energy transition — pushing boundaries, turning bold ideas into reality, and solving the problems that matter most.

* Curious and a natural inclination to explore new technologies, methodologies, and approaches, driving continuous improvement; actively seizing opportunities and taking initiative.

**Why join us?** 




 At Ingrid, we're pioneers at the forefront of the global energy transition — pushing boundaries, turning bold ideas into reality, and solving what matters most. Our culture is built on Bold Impact · Curious Grit · Inspiring Collaboration. We believe a thriving organisation runs on trust, growth, and learning from mistakes — and that wellbeing matters as much as work. From lunch & learns to social activities, we make space to connect, recharge, and thrive together.
 



  





**Next step** 




 We’ll make selections on an ongoing basis and finalise the recruitment as soon as we’ve found the perfect match. This process will run over summer vacations and we will keep everyone involved updated on our expected timeline.

 /Team Ingrid

title: Coor - Fullstackutvecklare
description:
**Nu söker vi dig som är fullstackutvecklare och har erfarenhet av att använda AI‑verktyg i ditt utvecklingsarbete!** 




  





**Vill du bidra med din kunskap i ett team som utvecklar och förvaltar digitala säkerhetslösningar, samtidigt som du blir en del av ett nordiskt företag med ett mindre och nära IT-säkerhetsteam? Här får du möjlighet att arbeta med utvecklingen av en digital tjänst inom säkerhet i en familjär miljö med korta beslutsvägar, där du kan påverka, bidra och vara med och driva utvecklingen framåt.** 




  





  





**Om Coor / Addici** 




**Coor** 
 är en av Nordens ledande leverantörer av Facility Management-tjänster med en stark position inom arbetsplatsservice, fastighet och säkerhet. Vi har en prestigelös och utvecklingsinriktad kultur som präglas av ansvar, självständighet och samarbete. Hos oss får du möjlighet att driva förändring och utveckling tillsammans med kunniga kollegor i ett stimulerande och professionellt klimat.
 



  





 Inom Coor verkar säkerhetsbolaget
 **Addici Security & Technology** 
 , som levererar innovativa lösningar inom service, bevakning och teknisk säkerhet. Addici kombinerar operativ säkerhet med modern teknik och utvecklar digitala lösningar för kunder med höga krav på tillgänglighet, regelefterlevnad och informationssäkerhet.
 



  





  





**Om rollen** 




 Hos oss blir du en viktig spelare i vidareutvecklingen och förvaltningen av vår egen digitala säkerhetslösning, SmartID. Detta är
 
 vår egenutvecklade tjänst för säker och effektiv hantering av passerkort och accesser med stöd för automatiserade processer, som minskar risken för obehörig åtkomst. Lösningen används av kunder inom energi-, bank- och försvarssektorn och ställer höga krav på robust arkitektur, säker kod och skalbarhet on-prem i kundernas egna IT-miljöer.
 



  





 Rollen spänner över hela utvecklingskedjan – från design och arkitektur, via utveckling och test, till driftsättning och support. Arbetet sker i nära samarbete med hela utvecklingsteamet samt med IT-driften och kundsupporten. För att trivas i rollen är du positiv, engagerad och prestigelös, med en naturlig vilja att dela kunskap, ta ansvar och bidra till ett starkt och välfungerande team.
 



  





  





**Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden** 



* Förvalta, förbättra och kvalitetssäkra befintlig kodbas
* Utveckla och implementera nya funktioner i SmartID-plattformen
* Delta i design, arkitektur och tekniska beslut
* Bygga och vidareutveckla integrationer och API:er mot externa system
* Implementera och supportera lösningen hos kund
* Säkerställa att lösningen uppfyller högt ställda säkerhetskrav
* Arbeta med testning



  





  





**Skallkrav** 



* Minst 5 års arbetslivserfarenhet inom systemutveckling
* Stark kompetens inom .NET/C# och JavaScript/TypeScript
* Goda kunskaper i React
* God erfarenhet av Microsoft SQL Server
* Erfarenhet av att bygga integrationer med och mot API:er
* Vana av versionshanteringssystem exv. Bitbucket eller liknande och etablerade utvecklingsflöden
* Erfarenhet av att använda AI‑verktyg (t.ex. kodassistenter, analys‑ eller teststöd) som ett naturligt stöd i det dagliga utvecklingsarbetet
* Erfarenhet av Visual Studio
* Erfarenhet av manuella tester
* Flytande svenska i tal och skrift
* Är trygg i sin tekniska kompetens, kommunikativ i dialog med kollegor och kund, samt har ett lösningsorienterat och förbättringsdrivet arbetssätt



  





*Rollen är placerad i säkerhetsklass och säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) kommer genomföras innan anställning. Svenskt medborgarskap är ett krav.* 




  





  





**Meriterande** 



* Erfarenhet av containerteknik (Docker/Kubernetes)
* Erfarenhet av Octopus Deploy
* Erfarenhet av EntityFramework
* Erfarenhet av att införa eller vidareutveckla AI-stöd i utvecklingsteam
* Erfarenhet av automatiserade tester
* Erfarenhet av arbete i säkerhetsklassade eller reglerade miljöer
* Tidigare erfarenhet i en ledande teknisk roll, där du t.ex. haft ansvar för arkitektur eller tekniska beslut
* Erfarenhet av säker utveckling samt att testa system genom egna penetrationstester



  





  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 




 Coor skapar några av Nordens bästa arbetsmiljöer – smarta, hållbara och inspirerande platser där människor trivs och kan prestera sitt bästa. För oss handlar service om mer än leverans; vi vill bygga starka team och lösningar som gör verklig skillnad och som ger våra kunder möjlighet att fokusera på det de gör bäst.
 



  





 Hos oss får du en betydelsefull roll i ett team som präglas av samarbete, respekt och engagemang. Vi arbetar i en platt organisation med korta beslutsvägar, där dina idéer tas på allvar och där du får goda möjligheter att påverka, ta ansvar och utvecklas vidare i din roll. Tillsammans skapar vi en arbetsmiljö som är både meningsfull och rolig att vara en del av.
 



  




* Fri parkering för alla anställda
* Tillgång till gym vid kontoret till en låg avgift
* Kollektivanslutna
* Erbjuder möjlighet till löneväxling
* Arbetstidsförkortning enligt företagets modell
* Utbildningsmöjligheter och utveckling inom företaget



  





 Tjänsten utgår från vårt kontor i Kista, med möjlighet till hybridarbete.
 



  





  





**Ansökningsprocessen & kontaktuppgifter** 




 Urvalsarbete sker löpande. Välkommen med din ansökan!
 



  





**Placering:** 
 Kista, Stockholm (hybrid)
 



**Start:** 
 Omgående eller enligt överenskommelse.
 



  





  





**Infinity HR** 
 är framgångsrika inom rekrytering av spetskvalitet till kulturstarka bolag och Coors partner i denna rekrytering. För mer info om tjänsten eller urvalsprocessen, vänligen kontakta Klara Olsson, klara.olsson@infinityitc.se 076-0565107.

title: Senior Backend Developer
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a
 **Senior Backend Developer** 
 in our
 **Stockholm** 
 office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?
   

  

**The Role**
 You’ll join a platform team at the center of a transformation of our commercial backend. We build and maintain a standardized, flexible, and resilient commerce foundation that reduces technical complexity, helping our product teams move faster and be more creative.
   

  

**In Practice, That Means**
* Simplifying a complex legacy landscape
* Building modern services alongside it
* Creating clear APIs, patterns, and capabilities that other teams can rely on
* Making it easier, not harder, for teams to build and experiment


**What You'll Do**
* Take ownership from problem → solution → production → iteration
* Make pragmatic technical decisions that balance speed, quality, and business impact
* Collaborate closely with multiple teams - this is a highly cross-team role
* Contribute to evolving our platform from legacy to modern, modular solutions
* Leverage AI tools where it adds value, from accelerating development to improving quality and learning faster


**What We’re Looking For**
 We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following:
   

  

* Strong backend experience (Node.js, Rust, or similar)
* Experience working with cloud platforms (preferably AWS)
* Experience contributing to refactoring or modernization efforts
* Experience working across team boundaries


**It’s a bonus if you have:** 
  

  

* Experience from a platform or enablement team
* Experience in high-traffic/transactional systems
* Experience working in ambiguous or fast-changing environments


**You’ll Thrive Here If You**
* Have a bias for action - you don’t wait for perfect clarity before starting to deliver
* Are pragmatic - you know when something is “good enough” and iterate from there
* Are comfortable working with complex or legacy systems
* Take ownership and drive your work forward independently
* Enjoy collaborating and enabling other teams to succeed
* Are comfortable in a fluid, evolving environment
* Are curious about new tools and ways of working


 What we offer you
   

  

* We’ve got you covered! 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
* Your wellbeing matters. We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
* Flexibility that works. We follow a hybrid model with at least three days a week in the office, giving you the best mix of collaboration and focus time.
* A safe space to grow and up-skill. Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
* An innovative environment with Hack Days once a year. This week-long initiative allows you to think outside the box and deliver creative, technical solutions that (more often than not) go on to be implemented, either in our product or our ways of working.
* Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do. So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including serier & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.


**Hit play today**
 If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position.
   

  

 Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: Full Stack Engineer
description:
Equal IT are partnered with a fast-growing, well-established SaaS company based in Stockholm.
 



  





 Their enterprise software is trusted by global brands, and right now they are making a genuine investment in embedding AI across the entire engineering org, not just baking it into the product.
 



  





 We are looking for a Senior Engineer to join one of their autonomous product teams. You will have real ownership from architectural decisions through to what ships and how it performs in production, and you will be expected to bring others up with you, not just write good code solo.
 



  





**What you will be working with:** 



* TypeScript / Angular on the frontend (experience from other frameworks is fine)
* C# / .NET on the backend
* MongoDB, Azure, Kubernetes
* Full-stack, backend-heavy, or frontend-heavy are all welcome. They care more about depth than which side of the stack you lean toward.



  





**What we are looking for:** 



* 5+ years building and shipping production software
* Genuine ownership mentality: you don't wait to be told what to fix
* Comfortable with pair/mob programming, and you enjoy helping less experienced engineers level up
* You actually use AI tools in your day-to-day work and are curious about where it goes next, not just aware they exist



  





**The setup:** 



* Hybrid — 3 days/week in a central Stockholm office
* Competitive salary, pension (ITP1), health allowance, private healthcare, parental leave top-up
* Small, autonomous teams — not a 200-person engineering org where you are a cog



  





 Think this could be a good fit? Let's have a quick chat. I can give you the full picture so you can decide whether it's worth taking things further.

title: Senior Backend Developer (Python/DevOps)
description:
Are you an experienced Backend or Fullstack Developer who enjoys bridging the gap between robust code and cutting-edge tech? Do you want the unique possibility to invest in the next generation of tech startups while building large-scale solutions?
   

  

 Then you might be the right fit for VNTRS!
   

  

 Location: Stockholm (Onsite/Hybrid)
   

  

 Our Vision & Mission
   

  

 Our vision is to be the ultimate partner for building the tech companies of the future. We transform innovative ideas into thriving businesses. As an employer, our goal is to provide an environment where technical excellence meets entrepreneurship, and where your personal growth drives our collective success.
   

  

 Your Impact: Builder & Investor
   

  

 At VNTRS, you are more than a developer; you are a builder and an investor,
   

  

**Strategic Growth** 
 : Gain deep business acumen by working across both the startup ecosystem and the enterprise world.
   

  

**Ownership & Equity:** 
 At VNTRS, we combine the impact of high-level consulting with the strategic upside of venture capital. Through our unique model, you gain ownership in the companies we support, becoming a shareholder in our diverse portfolio of over 35 ventures.
   

  

**The Investment Council:** 
 No matter your current project, you are always a core part of VNTRS. You’ll for example have the opportunity to join our Investment Council and help decide which companies we back next.
   

  

**AI Landscape:** 
 We encourage initiative and provide the space to turn your curiosity about AI/ML into real-world growth.
   

  

 The Role: Tech & Innovation
   

  

 You will join our Tech Forum, a collaborative group of experts (Fullstack, DevOps, Architects) dedicated to high-quality code and modern solutions.
   

  

**Core Backend:** 
 Design and develop scalable systems primarily in Python with frameworks such as django, flask or fastapi.
   

  

**DevOps & Infrastructure:** 
 Take ownership of CI/CD pipelines and cloud environments using GCP (preferred), AWS, Docker, and Terraform.
   

  

**Dynamic Projects** 
 : Toggle between high-stakes projects, moving seamlessly from building products from the ground up to solving complex challenges for large enterprises.
   

  

 Culture & People
   

  

 Our culture is built on our values: Adventurous, Caring, and Inspiring.
   

  

**Community** 
 : We are a dog-friendly office that loves spending time together through Hackathons, After Works, and communal lunches.
   

  

**Balance:** 
 We respect that everyone is in different stages of life. Your well-being and work-life balance are top priorities.
   

  

**Growth Mindset:** 
 We offer a dedicated education budget and a clear path for career progression.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 Experience:
 **5-7 years** 
 of professional backend or fullstack development.
   

  

 Technical Stack: High proficiency in
 **Python (e.g., Django, flask, fastapi)**
 Cloud infrastructure: Preferably
 **GCP or AWS.**
**DevOps** 
 : CI/CD Pipelines, Infrastructure as Code (IaC) (e.g. Terraform, Ansible), Microservices containerization (e.g. Docker/Kubernetes)
   

  

**Consultancy Mindset:** 
 You have experience working as a consultant or across various projects and thrive in the journey from Startup to Enterprise.
   

  

**Business Acumen:** 
 You are interested in the "why" behind the code and how it creates value.
   

  

 Language: Fluent in
 **Swedish and English.**
**AI - No need to be an expert, but a keen interest with some experience:**
 Agent-Native: You integrate AI agents and autonomous workflows as a natural part of your backend architecture and infra-stack.
   

  

 Master of Tools: You’ve moved beyond simple chats. You leverage Cursor, Copilot, and advanced agents to accelerate delivery without ever compromising on code quality.
   

  

 Scalable AI: You know how to build and grow AI features in a fullstack environment while keeping the system stable and fast.
   

  

**Nice To Have**
 Frontend: Experience or interest in collaborating with our experts in React/Next.js.
   

  

 Startup Experience: Experience building products from scratch.
   

  

 Ready to be more than just a developer? Apply now and help us build the next generation of successful companies, regardless of the project.

title: Staff Software Engineer
description:
**Who We Are**
 Helsing is a defence AI company. Our mission is to protect our democracies. We aim to achieve technological leadership, so that open societies can continue to make sovereign decisions and control their ethical standards.
   

  

 As democracies, we believe we have a special responsibility to be thoughtful about the development and deployment of powerful technologies like AI. We take this responsibility seriously.
   

  

 We are an ambitious and committed team of engineers, AI specialists and customer-facing programme managers. We are looking for mission-driven people to join our European teams – and apply their skills to solve the most complex and impactful problems. We embrace an open and transparent culture that welcomes healthy debates on the use of technology in defence, its benefits, and its ethical implications.
   

  

**The role**
 At Helsing, our engineering culture is defined by technical impact, first-principles thinking, and high-agency execution. We are looking for a hands-on Staff Software Engineer to serve as a primary technical anchor, driving architectural decisions and shipping code across our backend systems.
   

  

 Designed for a builder who thrives on complexity and wants to remain close to the metal, you will have the autonomy to own projects from zero to one prototyping through to deploying resilient software on physical hardware.
   

  

 Your impact is measured by your ability to design robust systems, write performance-optimised code, and act as a technical multiplier for our teams. You will architect solutions across highly resilient distributed systems built for network partitions, resource-constrained embedded hardware, secure networking protocols, and high-performance machine learning inference pipelines directly at the tactical edge.
   

  

**The day-to-day**
* Lead the review of complex code contributions, design proposals, and technical RFCs from colleagues. At Helsing we strongly believe that unblocking others and mentoring is the highest-leverage work you can do.
* Architect, propose, and implement major new features, capabilities, algorithms, infrastructure, or full products across our stack. We expect our engineers to look beyond their immediate team, identify systemic opportunities, and champion the solutions.
* Act as a key translator between engineering and the broader business. You will regularly interface with product managers, programme leads, and customers to translate deep technical designs and constraints into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders who depend on our technologies.
* Actively mentor engineers, find mentees, and help drive and scale our technical interviewing pipeline. You will be a key driver in fostering, growing, and scaling the engineering organisation.
* Share your extensive experience and technical know-how with other Helsingers. We host tech talks, run "let’s learn together" groups, and host topical office hours (e.g., on Rust) to help the entire engineering team get better with time.
* Maintain and evolve our existing software stack, partnering with internal and external stakeholders to diagnose complex system issues, optimise operational reliability and performance, and systematically address technical debt. Our stack is a mix of Rust and Python, with Python mainly living in the ML-heavy sections.
* Design and develop robust test architectures and benchmarks, both at the micro and macro level. We operate in a domain where robustness is of the utmost importance, and building highly reliable, performance-tested systems is key to achieving that.
* Present and celebrate your work at our weekly demo sessions. We host demo sessions that allow early feedback, celebration of progress, and a better shared understanding of everything that goes on at Helsing.


**You should apply if you**
* Have a proven track record of writing highly legible, maintainable, and performance-optimised systems code.
* Are a systems-level thinker capable of designing for the long term. You can ensure that a complex collection of independent, heterogeneous systems (including those outside your direct control) successfully integrate and function reliably end-to-end.
* Communicate well in English, both verbally and in writing, with the ability to articulate complex architectural decisions to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
* Thrive in a collaborative, low-ego environment, with extensive experience running pair programming and peer code reviews.
* Are highly comfortable working asynchronously (writing robust PRs, leading design RFCs, and having discussions by email).
* Have seasoned, senior-to-staff level backend development experience, with expert-level mastery in a systems language like Rust, Java, Go, or C++.
* Are a natural multiplier, striving to constantly improve yourself, your peers, and the engineering culture around you.
* Feel like ethical considerations are an inherent part of software engineering.


*Note: We operate at an intersection where women, as well as other minority groups, are systematically under-represented. We encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all the listed qualifications; ability and impact cannot be summarised in a few bullet points.*
**Nice to have**
* Production-grade Rust and Python specifically.
* Container-based and cloud-native application architectures (e.g., Docker, Azure, AWS).
* Metrics and observability tooling (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK).
* SQL and high-throughput streaming database systems.
* Low-level routing protocols (e.g., IGP, BGP).
* Cryptographic protocols (e.g., Signal, WireGuard).
* Hardware interfaces (e.g., Arduino).
* Robotics (e.g., control systems).
* Production ML systems and model deployment.


**Join Helsing and work with world-leading experts in their fields**
* Helsing’s work is important. You’ll be directly contributing to the protection of democratic countries while balancing both ethical and geopolitical concerns.
* The work is unique. We operate in a domain that has highly unusual technical requirements and constraints, and where robustness, safety, and ethical considerations are vital. You will face unique Engineering and AI challenges that make a meaningful impact in the world.
* Our work frequently takes us right up to the state of the art in technical innovation, be it reinforcement learning, distributed systems, generative AI, or deployment infrastructure. The defence industry is entering the most exciting phase of the technological development curve. Advances in our field of world are not incremental: Helsing is part of, and often leading, historic leaps forward.
* In our domain, success is a matter of order-of-magnitude improvements and novel capabilities. This means we take bets, aim high, and focus on big opportunities. Despite being a relatively young company, Helsing has already been selected for multiple significant government contracts.
* We actively encourage healthy, proactive, and diverse debate internally about what we do and how we choose to do it. Teams and individual engineers are trusted (and encouraged) to practise responsible autonomy and critical thinking, and to focus on outcomes, not conformity. At Helsing you will have a say in how we (and you!) work, the opportunity to engage on what does and doesn’t work, and to take ownership of aspects of our culture that you care deeply about.


**What We Offer**
* A focus on outcomes, not time-tracking
* Competitive compensation and VSOP options
* Relocation support
* Social and education allowances
* Regular company events and all-hands to bring together employees as one team across Europe
* A hands-on onboarding program (affectionately labelled “Infraduction”), in which you will be building tooling and applications to be used across the company. This is your opportunity to learn our tech stack, explore the company, and learn how we get things done - all whilst working with other engineering teams from day one


*Helsing is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability or gender identity. Please do not submit personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, data concerning your health, or data concerning your sexual orientation.*
 Helsing's Candidate Privacy and Confidentiality Regime can be found here.

title: Back End Developer (AI Infrastructure)
description:
**About The Role**
 What if the code you write could directly power the AI systems shaping how millions of people interact with technology? We're looking for Back End Developers in Stockholm to design, build, and optimize the server-side systems and APIs that drive cutting-edge AI products — from data pipelines and model-serving infrastructure to scalable microservices that handle real-world traffic at scale.
   

  

 This is a fully remote, flexible contract role. Whether you're a seasoned engineer looking for meaningful project work or a strong developer ready to apply your skills to AI-adjacent infrastructure, this is your chance to build things that matter.
   

  

* Organization: Alignerr
* Type: Hourly Contract
* Location: Remote
* Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


**What You'll Do**
* Design, develop, and maintain robust back end services, APIs, and data pipelines
* Build scalable, high-performance server-side architecture to support AI training and inference workloads
* Write clean, well-documented, and testable code following engineering best practices
* Integrate with databases, message queues, cloud services, and third-party APIs
* Optimize system performance, reliability, and scalability as usage grows
* Collaborate asynchronously with distributed teams of engineers, researchers, and product leads
* Troubleshoot production issues, conduct code reviews, and contribute to architectural decisions
* Work independently on task-based and project-based assignments on your own schedule


**Who You Are**
* Proficient in one or more back end languages — Python, Go, Java, Node.js, Rust, or similar
* Solid understanding of RESTful API design, microservices architecture, and distributed systems
* Experienced with relational and/or NoSQL databases (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, etc.)
* Comfortable working with cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, or Azure
* Familiar with version control (Git), CI/CD pipelines, and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)
* Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to debug complex systems independently
* Self-motivated, reliable, and effective when working asynchronously without close supervision
* Clear communicator who can document decisions and collaborate across time zones


**Nice to Have**
* Experience building infrastructure for machine learning or AI systems
* Familiarity with data processing frameworks (Apache Kafka, Spark, Airflow, or similar)
* Background in performance optimization, caching strategies, or high-throughput systems
* Knowledge of authentication, authorization, and security best practices
* Experience with serverless architectures or event-driven design patterns
* Contributions to open-source projects or a strong portfolio of personal/professional work


**Why Join Us**
* Work on cutting-edge AI infrastructure projects alongside leading research labs
* Fully remote and flexible — set your own hours and work from anywhere
* Freelance autonomy with access to meaningful, technically challenging work
* Build systems at the intersection of software engineering and artificial intelligence
* Collaborate with a global network of talented engineers and researchers
* Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Senior Genesys Developer
description:

title: Senior Python Developer to Husqvarna Group
description:
**Last date to apply:**
 01 August 2026
   

  

 At Husqvarna Group, we are not only building innovative outdoor power tools — we are also transforming into a data-driven, digitally empowered company. Our IT and data landscape spans across continents, cloud platforms and business areas, and we’re on a mission to make data a true asset - at every level of the organization.
   

  

 With a legacy of innovation dating back to 1689 — from sewing machines and motorcycles to chainsaws and robotic lawnmowers. Husqvarna has always evolved with the times. Today, that evolution is digital. and the future is powered by data.
   

  

 Now, we’re looking for a
 **Senior Python Developer** 
 to strengthen our data foundation and help take our internal tools to the next level.
   

  

**About This Opportunity**
 You’ll be joining a small and highly skilled core team of three that, over the past five years, has developed and nurtured Husqvarna’s data mesh platform. The team supports and guides more than 35 development teams across both Azure and AWS — ensuring scalable, secure and high-quality data solutions across the Group.
   

  

 In this role, you’ll take the lead on developing and maintaining our internal tool Data Registry — the backbone of how we manage and govern data across the organization. You’ll design web applications, build pipelines, and contribute to a strong data engineering foundation that enables innovation at scale.
   

  

**Your role**
 As Senior Python Developer, you will:
   

  

* Design, develop and maintain web applications using Python and Django
* Create and maintain pipelines in Databricks using PySpark
* Be responsible for security of our web application and AWS account(s)
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design and ship new features
* Implement robust security and data protection mechanisms
* Stay up-to-date with new technologies and innovations in web and data development


**About You**
 You’ll thrive in this role if you enjoy combining technical depth with collaboration. You like building things that last — robust, elegant and well-documented solutions others can depend on.
   

  

 You’re curious by nature, humble in your approach and self-driven in execution. You take ownership, listen to your colleagues, and keep an open mind to new ideas. Above all, you care about quality and clarity — both in your code and your communication.
   

  

**Your technical toolbox**
**Must-have skills:**
* Python – deep knowledge of Python and its ecosystem
* Django – solid experience building web applications
* Web fundamentals – HTML, CSS, JavaScript
* AWS – hands-on experience with services such as CloudFormation, ECS, and S3
* Infrastructure as Code (IaC) – familiarity with tools like Terraform or AWS CloudFormation
* Nice-to-have / merited skills:
* Databricks – experience building and maintaining data pipelines
* Azure – exposure to Azure services and data tools
* DevOps practices – experience automating and optimizing development workflows


**Location**
 This position can be based either in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, or at one of our sites in Sweden.
   

  

**How We Work**
 We believe the best ideas happen when we're connected. We spend most of our time together in the office, creating space for collaboration, creativity, and fast decision-making. This is consistent across all our global offices and helps us maintain a strong culture, support learning and development, and ensure everyone has access to the people and resources they need to thrive.
   

  

**Your application**
 We look forward to receiving your application! Please note that selection is ongoing, and the position may be filled before the final application date. For questions about the process, contact
 **Talent Acquisition Partner Omkar Phadke** 
 at omkar.phadke@husqvarnagroup.com or
 **Talent Acquisition Partner Victoria Kjellgren** 
 at victoria.kjellgren@husqvarnagroup.com
   

  

**About Husqvarna Group:** 
  

  

*Husqvarna Group is a global leader in innovative solutions for managing forests, parks, and gardens, as well as cutting equipment and diamond tools for the light construction and stone industries. With an innovative mindset, we are dedicated to deliver high-quality solutions ranging from robotic mowers to chainsaws, watering systems and power cutters, with a strong focus on our customers and future generations.* 
  

  

*Founded in the Swedish town Huskvarna in 1689, we have been pioneers in our business for more than three centuries. Today, we are mainly operating under the Husqvarna and Gardena brands, serving consumers and professionals in over 100 countries through direct sales, dealers, and retailers. Headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, Husqvarna Group employs approximately 13,300 people in 40 countries and reported net sales of SEK 48,4 billion in 2024.* 
*Husqvarna Group is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm*

title: Senior Product Developer
description:
**About TOTEME** 




 TOTEME is a fashion house rooted in Swedish sensibilities, modern aspirations and the notion of style. With an appreciation for women’s many roles and how pieces are worn in practice, we create emblematic designs with an emphasis on materiality and shape. Representing a direct and decisive way of dressing, the collections are contextualized in curated edits, visuals and spaces.
 



  





 TOTEME was founded in 2014 by Elin Kling and Karl Lindman. From the atelier in Stockholm, we create collections spanning ready-to-wear, shoes, bags, accessories and jewelry.
 



  





**The Role** 




 TOTEME is looking for a Senior Product Developer to join our team in Stockholm full-time. In this role, you will be responsible for developing products from sketch handover through to shipment sample sign-off for our apparel collection. Working across categories including, but not limited to, leather- and outerwear, you will report to the Head of Product Development RTW and collaborate closely with the Design, Pattern, Technical, Production, and Raw Material teams to ensure seamless product execution.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 



* Work closely with the Design and Product teams to develop each garment from initial concept through to finished product, ensuring alignment with brand vision and commercial objectives.
* Act as a trusted technical partner to the Design team, providing hands-on leadership in fittings and reviews to drive best-in-class fit, quality, and product performance.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with manufacturers, negotiating costs, lead times, and technical solutions to uphold design intent while meeting business targets.
* Ensure that developed products are fit for purpose for the customer, the brand, and the business, balancing aesthetic, functionality, quality, and margin.
* Identify potential risks and challenges across all stages of development and production, proactively proposing solutions with a focus on quality, pricing, and margin optimisation.
* Continuously refine development processes to improve product quality, efficiency, and cost effectiveness.
* Manage timelines by meeting deadlines and clearly communicating key milestones to cross-functional teams.
* Accurately maintain all product data in the PLM system throughout the development process.
* Deliver products in line with TOTEME’s sustainability standards and values, ensuring relevant product data is consistently updated and accessible for the sustainability team.



  





**Your Profile** 



* Extensive experience in multi category product development, ideally including leather- and outerwear experience.
* Proven ability to partner with senior designers, interpreting creative vision, and translating it to clear technical and commercial direction for teams and vendors.
* Skilled in managing supplier relationships to deliver against quality, cost and timing requirements.
* Experienced with managing the critical path, ensuring collections are delivered on time.
* Able to manage a budget with commercial awareness and understanding of product cost, achieving a balance between creativity and commercial implications throughout the process.
* Demonstrates expert-level technical product knowledge.
* Excellent knowledge of PLM systems, Microsoft Office and Adobe programs.
* Fluent in spoken and written English.



  





**About You** 



* A TOTEME ambassador who lives and breathes our culture and values.
* Excellent communicator with the ability to negotiate with credibility, share information effectively, and collaborate confidently with cross-functional teams.
* Well-informed about industry trends, the market, luxury consumers, and competitors.
* Highly organized with strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
* Positive and proactive mindset, with a can-do attitude.
* Able to work independently and as part of a team.



  





 This is a full-time position based at our Stockholm Head office, starting as soon as possible.
 



 TOTEME has teams in Stockholm, London, New York, and Shanghai, as well as retail spaces across Europe, North America, and Asia.
 



  





 TOTEME has an internal career development and title framework and the job title used in the recruitment ad may differ from the title used internally at TOTEME.
 



  





 Candidates progress through application, screening, interviews or case assessments, followed by final pre-employment checks and a formal offer before joining our team. We anonymize names and personal details in applications during the screening stage to support fair and skills-based hiring.
 



  





*By applying to this position, I agree with TOTEME’s privacy policy and to be contacted about future job opportunities for up to two years from the submission date. Find out more about our privacy policy
 
 here
 
 .*

title: Senior Software Engineer (AI Infrastructure)
description:
**About The Role**
 What if your engineering skills could directly shape the platforms and systems behind the AI revolution? We're looking for Senior Software Engineers in Stockholm and across Sweden to design, build, and scale the infrastructure that powers cutting-edge AI products used by millions of people worldwide.
   

  

 This is high-impact, technically challenging work — the kind that keeps great engineers engaged. You'll tackle real architectural problems, write code that matters, and collaborate with some of the sharpest minds in AI research and engineering.
   

  

 This is a fully remote, flexible contract role. If you're an experienced engineer who thrives on autonomy, writes clean and scalable code, and wants to work at the frontier of technology — we want to hear from you.
   

  

* Organization: Alignerr
* Type: Hourly Contract
* Location: Remote
* Commitment: 20–40 hours/week


**What You'll Do**
* Design, develop, and maintain production-grade software systems that support AI training, evaluation, and deployment pipelines
* Write clean, well-tested, and performant code in Python, TypeScript, or other modern languages
* Build and optimize APIs, data pipelines, and backend services that operate reliably at scale
* Architect solutions that balance speed of delivery with long-term maintainability
* Troubleshoot complex technical issues across distributed systems and cloud infrastructure
* Collaborate asynchronously with cross-functional teams — including ML engineers, researchers, and product managers
* Participate in code reviews and contribute to engineering best practices across the team
* Work independently on well-scoped projects with meaningful ownership and autonomy


**Who You Are**
* 5+ years of professional software engineering experience
* Strong proficiency in at least one modern programming language — Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, Go, Java, or similar
* Solid understanding of software architecture, design patterns, and system design principles
* Experience building and deploying backend services, APIs, or data-intensive applications
* Comfortable working with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and containerized environments
* Familiar with version control (Git), CI/CD workflows, and modern development tooling
* Strong debugging and problem-solving skills — you can navigate ambiguity and find root causes efficiently
* Excellent written communication skills — you can document decisions, explain tradeoffs, and collaborate effectively in an async-first environment
* Self-directed and reliable — you manage your own time and deliver consistently without close supervision


**Nice to Have**
* Experience with ML/AI infrastructure — training pipelines, model serving, data annotation platforms, or evaluation frameworks
* Familiarity with large-scale data processing tools (Spark, Kafka, Airflow, or similar)
* Background in distributed systems, microservices architecture, or event-driven design
* Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi) or Kubernetes
* Contributions to open-source projects or a public portfolio of technical work
* Previous experience in a remote-first or async-heavy engineering culture


**Why Join Us**
* Work on cutting-edge AI infrastructure alongside world-class research labs and engineering teams
* Fully remote and flexible — set your own schedule and work from anywhere
* Tackle genuinely hard technical problems with real-world impact at scale
* High autonomy and ownership — no micromanagement, just meaningful engineering work
* Collaborate with a global community of top-tier engineers and researchers
* Potential for ongoing work, expanded scope, and long-term contract extension as projects grow

title: iOS Developer
description:
**Role: iOS Developer** 




**Location: Stockholm, Sweden** 




**Type: Contract** 




  





**Job Description:** 




 We are looking for a highly skilled Senior Software Engineer (iOS) to design, develop, and maintain high-quality iOS applications. In this role, you will collaborate closely with product managers, designers, engineering teams, and other stakeholders to deliver scalable, performant, and user-centric mobile experiences while adhering to modern software engineering best practices.
 



**Core Technologies** 



* Swift
* SwiftUI
* Objective-C
* UIKit
* iOS SDK
* Architecture & Design Patterns (MVVM, VIPER, Clean Architecture)
* Unit Testing
* REST APIs
* Performance Optimization
* CI/CD and DevOps practices



**Key Responsibilities** 



* Design, develop, and maintain scalable iOS applications using Swift and SwiftUI.
* Lead technical design discussions and contribute to architectural decisions that promote maintainability, scalability, and performance.
* Collaborate with product managers, designers, QA engineers, backend developers, and cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality features.
* Mentor and support junior engineers through technical guidance, code reviews, and knowledge sharing.
* Ensure adherence to coding standards, best practices, and engineering processes.
* Identify and resolve complex technical challenges related to performance, memory management, concurrency, and application stability.
* Optimize application performance using profiling and debugging tools.
* Integrate mobile applications with backend services and RESTful APIs.
* Participate in Agile development processes, including sprint planning, estimation, and retrospectives.
* Stay current with the latest advancements in iOS development and recommend appropriate technologies, frameworks, and engineering practices.



**Required Skills & Qualifications** 




**Swift & SwiftUI** 



* Strong expertise in Swift and SwiftUI, with the ability to build complex, responsive, and maintainable user interfaces.
* Deep understanding of Swift language features, modern APIs, and best practices.



**iOS Development** 



* Extensive experience with the iOS SDK and core Apple frameworks, including:
* UIKit
* Core Data
* Core Animation
* Core Graphics
* Strong understanding of application lifecycle, memory management, and concurrency.



**Software Architecture** 



* Proven experience implementing scalable architectures such as MVVM, VIPER, or Clean Architecture.
* Ability to design modular, testable, and maintainable applications.



**Networking** 



* Experience integrating RESTful APIs.
* Strong understanding of asynchronous programming, JSON parsing, authentication, and error handling.



**Performance & Debugging** 



* Proficiency with Xcode debugging tools and Instruments.
* Experience diagnosing performance bottlenecks, crash analysis, and memory optimization.



**Testing & Quality** 



* Experience writing unit tests and promoting testable application design.
* Commitment to code quality through peer reviews and engineering best practices.

title: Senior/Staff+ Software Engineer
description:
**Senior/Staff+ Software Engineer** 




 Build the systems that scale video advertising on the open web. Get the workflow right first, then use AI to help it run.
 



 Most ad budgets flow to a few closed platforms for one reason: they make it easy. Buying video on the open web should be just as easy. At SeenThis, we're turning years of hands-on campaign delivery into products that automate the right work while keeping people in control.
 



 This is a senior-to-staff+ role for engineers who can take real product problems from ambiguity to production. You'll work across distributed systems, product workflows, LLM-powered automation, and the operational details that make the whole thing reliable at scale.
 



  





**About SeenThis** 




 The open web has massive untapped advertising potential, but video has historically underperformed here. SeenThis is changing that. As a video advertising partner, we prove the open web delivers exceptional results, capturing more attention with cost-effective video that keeps publishers profitable and the internet independent. Since 2017, we've served billions of streams for 5,000+ brands across 50+ countries. We believe brands deserve reach wherever audiences are, publishers deserve sustainable revenue, and the internet deserves to stay open.
 



  





 Our team of 130+ employees works globally from offices in Stockholm, Oslo, London, Paris, Singapore, Hong Kong, New York, and Sydney. We're expanding across all teams and constantly seeking new colleagues who want to make real impact in a fast-moving, collaborative environment where innovation happens daily.
 



 Do you want to be part of our journey, where we dare to discover, own it, and win together?
 



 Join SeenThis!
 



  





**About the role** 




 You'll join our R&D org to own problems end to end with your team. We re-team when it makes sense and work across the portfolio, so this role is not locked to one product area. Where you start, and where you grow next, depends on your strengths, interests, and where the business needs senior ownership most.
 



 The three streams you may work across are:
 



  





**Core Tech.** 
 The streaming, delivery, and measurement technology at the foundation of everything we do, handling billions of events a day.
 



**Studio** 
 . Our creative production tool, where teams build, adapt, and launch campaigns, and the engine behind our own managed production.
 



**Creative Manager.** 
 The orchestration layer and system of record for media plans, orders, approvals, creative delivery, and reporting.
 



 You'll work in a small team of peers: flat, fast, close to customers, close to the model, and serious about quality. You own problems, not tickets, and you own them together. We measure ourselves on whether what we ship changes behaviour.
 



 We're hiring across senior to staff+ level. The level follows your experience and the scope you're ready to own, and we calibrate it with you.
 



  





**Applied AI is central to the role,** 
 but it comes second on purpose. We get the product and workflow right for people first, with solid engineering and a strong human-in-the-loop experience. Then we apply AI to automate the work, one proven step at a time. The goal isn't to add AI for its own sake. It's to build systems where the product handles more of the work, people set intent, review exceptions, and stay in control.
 



 Agents can now take on a meaningful share of implementation work, which changes the job. The value is in judgment: framing the problem, setting the guardrails, and knowing what good looks like, so you can tell solid work from a confident mistake. We already build this way, with agents on most tasks through Claude Code and Codex, in-house skills that teach them our conventions, and an AI reviewer on every pull request.
 



  





**What you'll do** 



* Take a problem from discovery through to something shipped and working, with your team, and stay with it through launch and the iteration after.
* Own the full lifecycle: design, build, ship, operate, and iterate. Weigh tradeoffs in the open, make the architectural calls, and bring peers with you.
* Design systems that scale: distributed services where a dropped or double-counted event becomes a billing error, with the observability and operational care to keep them healthy.
* Build AI into real product workflows: evals, guardrails, tracing, human-in-the-loop review, and the state and tool contracts behind agents that hold up in production.
* Raise the engineering bar through code review, mentoring, technical direction, and the way you work.



  





**Qualifications** 



* **Senior level:** 
 you've owned complex systems end to end and shipped them into production.
* **Staff+ level:** 
 you've led ambiguous, multi-month technical problems, made strong architectural calls, and brought others with you.
* You have very strong engineering fundamentals. You design distributed systems that scale, weigh tradeoffs, and instrument for observability.
* You have full-stack range. We build in TypeScript and Node, but fundamentals matter more than the stack. You pick up what the problem needs.
* You've shipped LLM or agent features in production, beyond basic prompting: tool use, retrieval, orchestration, evals, and guardrails. You have an intuition for where models are reliable and where they need constraints, review, or another approach entirely.
* You drive alignment across technical and non-technical people, and work out what matters most rather than waiting for a spec.
* You have high ownership and low ego. You care about quality, lift the people around you, and want the work to matter.
* You already work AI-native: you direct agents, judge their output, and build your own tooling, while still being able to reason through a problem and debug without them. We'll ask for concrete examples of how you work this way.



  





**Bonus if you have experience with** 



* The platform layer behind an AI product: the orchestration, eval infrastructure, and tracing that other engineers build on, not only the features on top.
* Partnering with data or ML teams to productise model capabilities, or diagnosing and fixing model failure modes in production.
* High-throughput event pipelines, streaming data, or real-time systems.
* Our wider stack: BigQuery, ClickHouse, Airflow, and a multi-cloud footprint across AWS and GCP.
* Third-party platform APIs, such as Meta, ad servers, or DSPs, integrated and running in production.
* Ad tech, martech, creative tooling, or another commercially messy domain.



  





**Application** 




 If you're close on most of this and strong where it counts, we'd rather hear from you than not.
 



 As we review applications and interview on a continuous basis, please apply as soon as possible. Due to the high volume of applications received, we are unable to provide personalized feedback prior to the interview stage. Thank you for understanding!
 



  





**Please note:** 
 we're in our summer holiday period. Response times may be a little longer than usual, and we will get back to all candidates with feedback once the holiday period is over. Thank you for your understanding and patience!
 



  





 We conduct background checks on all SeenThis-hires to comply with our customers and protect business-critical information.
 



 We look forward to welcoming you to SeenThis!

title: Business IT Solution Developer - Credit & AML
description:
TRATON Finans AB is a financial services company within the business area TRATON Financial Services AB. We offer financing and insurance of Scania & MAN products through our seven branches in the Nordic and Baltic countries. With a customer finance portfolio of approximately SEK 27 billion, TRATON Finans AB is one of the largest financial services companies within TRATON Financial Services. The company has around 130 employees, of which half are located at the headquarters in Södertälje.
   

  

**Role Summary**
 Do you want to shape the future of digital finance solutions at TRATON Financial Services?
   

  

 We are looking for a
 **Business IT Solution Developer – Credit & AML** 
 to join our Process & IT Support team. In this role, you will act as the link between business and technology, driving the development, enhancement, and support of critical Credit and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) solutions used across our Nordic and Baltic markets.
   

  

 You will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, internal teams, and external software vendors to transform business needs into scalable and efficient IT solutions. Working within a modern and highly integrated application landscape, you will help ensure secure, compliant, and seamless customer and financing processes while contributing to the continuous improvement of our digital capabilities.
   

  

 As part of a diverse and collaborative team, you will have the opportunity to influence both technical solutions and business processes while working in an international environment that values innovation, ownership, and continuous learning.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Analyze business needs and translate them into functional and technical requirements.
* Design technical solutions together with internal stakeholders and external partners.
* Collaborate with vendors such as BanqSoft throughout development and implementation.
* Develop and maintain integrations, automations, and smaller application enhancements.
* Participate in testing, deployment, and production support.
* Ensure delivered solutions align with our architecture, quality standards, and business processes.
* Partner with the respective business departments to identify, prioritize, and implement improvements within the Credit & AML application landscape.


**Who You Are**
 We believe you bring a combination of technical expertise, business understanding, and a collaborative mindset. You enjoy solving complex problems, taking ownership of your work, and partnering with stakeholders to create practical and sustainable solutions.
   

  

 To be successful in this role, you likely have:
   

  

  

* A degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Systems Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
* Experience developing and maintaining business applications.
* Experience working with system integrations, APIs, and SQL databases.
* Experience gathering, analysing, and documenting business and technical requirements.
* Experience collaborating with external software vendors or development partners.
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to bridge business and technical perspectives.
* Fluency in English.


 It is considered an advantage if you have experience with:
   

  

  

* Credit, Financing, Leasing, or AML/KYC processes.
* Financial services or other regulated industries.
* BanqSoft solutions such as CoreView or Preview.
* Working in international and cross-functional environments.
* Swedish language skills.


 As a person, you are proactive, curious, and eager to learn. You thrive in a collaborative environment, enjoy taking responsibility, and are motivated by finding better ways of working and delivering value to the business.
   

  

**This Is Us**
 The Process & IT Support team is responsible for developing, supporting, and continuously improving the business-critical applications and processes that enable TRATON Finans' operations in the Nordics & Baltics.
   

  

 On the IT side, our Business Analysts, Solution Developers, Application Support Specialists, and Information Security function work closely with the business and external partners to deliver secure, efficient, and innovative solutions across sales, credit, AML, finance, insurance, and reporting.
   

  

 Alongside our IT responsibilities, we also have a dedicated team providing Central Administration & Support, managing operational services such as rental, factoring, and contract administration to ensure efficient day-to-day operations and high-quality customer service.
   

  

 Together, we combine business expertise, technical competence, and operational excellence to support Scania Finans throughout the entire business lifecycle.
   

  

**TRATON Offers**
 We believe great work starts with feeling supported, both professionally and personally. That’s why we offer a broad range of employee benefits designed to promote wellbeing, flexibility, and a healthy work-life balance. Employees can enjoy flexible working hours and a workplace that supports both collaboration and flexibility. With office Hubs in Södertälje and Stockholm, as well as coworking spaces at Sergels Torg and Kungens Kurva, you will have the best preconditions to pick the best workplace for your activities.
   

  

 We provide a yearly wellness contribution and you can train at our health center Gröndal, and you will access to occupational health services with doctors, nurses, and ergonomists. Mental health is important to us, therefore we also offer confidential staff support services for guidance related to personal challenges.
   

  

 In addition, employees benefit from pension plans, profit sharing programs, and extra time off or additional pension contributions. We also support family life through enhanced parental benefits and offer additional perks such as employee car leasing. Together, these benefits help create a workplace where you can thrive both at work and in everyday life.
   

  

**Application**
 We look forward to receiving your application, consisting of your CV and degree certificate(s). To ensure an efficient, fair and unbiased recruitment process for all parties, we kindly ask you not to submit a cover letter. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to fostering diversity throughout our recruitment processes.
   

  

 Apply as soon as possible, no later than 2026-08-07. Screening will take place on an ongoing basis during the application period. Logical and personality tests may be used as part of the selection process, and a background check may be required for this role.
   

  

 If you have questions or would like more information, please contact: Per Arvidsson, Operations Manager, per.arvidsson@scania.com
   

  

**We look forward to your application!**

title: Staff Machine Learning Engineer - Content Intelligence
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
   

  

 The Content Platform team powers the full lifecycle of content across music, podcasts, audiobooks, and emerging formats at Spotify. We ensure that everything from licensed catalog to user-generated content is trusted, safe, and high quality for millions of listeners worldwide. Our systems are responsible for how content is ingested, understood, enriched, governed, and distributed across the platform. As the scale and diversity of content continues to grow—driven by advances in AI and new creation tools—we’re building intelligent systems that can evaluate, manage, and route content reliably at global scale.
   

  

 We’re seeking a Staff Machine Learning Engineer to build and scale foundational ML systems that power content understanding across Spotify. In this role, you’ll work on systems that generate deep, machine-readable understanding of content across audio, video, text, and images—enabling automation, improving quality, and unlocking new product experiences. This work is central to delivering safe, high-quality, and differentiated experiences for millions of listeners and creators worldwide.
   

  

 What You Will Do
   

  

* Build and scale machine learning systems that generate deep understanding of content across modalities
* Develop models for classification, tagging, semantic understanding, and content enrichment
* Create high quality content enrichment at scale using LLMs and agentic systems.
* Design systems that make content intelligence signals available to downstream teams and products
* Improve automation for content quality, safety, and metadata enrichment at scale
* Collaborate with product, policy, and engineering teams to translate content intelligence into user impact
* Contribute to evaluation frameworks, data pipelines, and annotation systems
* Support rapid experimentation to prototype and launch new types of content signals
* Help improve system reliability, scalability, and performance across large datasets


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have experience building and deploying machine learning systems in production
* You are comfortable working with ML frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or similar
* You have experience working with large datasets and care about data quality and evaluation
* You are interested in or have worked with multimodal machine learning
* You understand how to design systems that balance automation with quality and user experience
* You are comfortable working on complex problems with evolving requirements
* You think in systems and understand how models connect to product outcomes
* You communicate clearly and work well across technical and non-technical teams


 Where You Will Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice
   

  

 Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Backendutvecklare (.NET/Java), Lumera
description:
*Vill du vara med på en av pensionsbranschens mest spännande transformationer? Som backend-utvecklare med erfarenhet av Lumera blir du en nyckelspelare i ett affärsnära integrationsteam som bygger framtidens lösningar. Här kombinerar du djup teknisk kompetens i både .NET och Java med förståelse för komplexa pensionsprodukter – i en miljö där moln, DevOps och modern arkitektur är en självklar del av vardagen.*
*På grund av semestertider kommer rekryteringsprocessen att påbörjas i augusti. Vi går då igenom inkomna ansökningar och återkopplar till samtliga kandidater.*
**Om jobbet**
 Vi befinner oss mitt i en omfattande teknisk och affärsmässig förändringsresa, där vi samtidigt introducerar ett nytt affärssystem (Lumera), genomför en förflyttning från Java till .NET och flyttar våra tjänster till molnet. Som backend-utvecklare kliver du in i hjärtat av denna transformation och säkerställer att våra integrationslösningar är robusta, skalbara och långsiktigt hållbara. Du blir en del av ett team som arbetar nära verksamheten med att knyta samman Lumera med kringliggande system och skapa en modern, effektiv och framtidssäker pensionsaffär.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer vara bland annat:
   

  

* Utveckla och förvalta integrationslösningar i både .NET och Java.
* Stötta förflyttningen från Java till .NET i både befintliga och nya tjänster.
* Integrera mot Lumera WS med både REST- och SOAP-baserade gränssnitt.
